source.title: Suspected neurological conditions: recognition and referral
source.population: Adults aged over 16 years with suspected neurological conditions.
source.setting: Initial assessment and referral from primary or community care.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected adult dizziness, vertigo, limb weakness, sensory symptoms, and tremor referral features; not applicable to children and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.7 — dizziness and vertigo in adults

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports focal neurological and hearing red flags, acute vestibular syndrome, HINTS by trained clinicians, neuroimaging referral, and Hallpike assessment for positional vertigo.

items.rationale: Exact phrase located in inspected full source.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-led outcome reassessment, persistent symptom documentation, safety discussion, questions, and arranged follow-up without generating treatment or a manoeuvre.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Benign Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo (Update)
source.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE vestibular and referral-pathway adaptation
source.population: Adults aged 18 years and older with suspected, diagnosed, monitored, or managed benign paroxysmal positional vertigo.
source.setting: Any clinical setting in which adult BPPV is identified, monitored, or managed.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult positional-symptom assessment, modifying factors, fall-risk context, reassessment, persistent symptoms, education, and clinician-arranged follow-up. It does not diagnose BPPV, select a manoeuvre, recommend imaging, or prescribe vestibular suppressants.

exact_location.heading: Key action statements 1a–3 — positional assessment, differential, and modifying factors

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting positional symptom context, clinician-performed diagnostic assessment, mobility, central nervous system context, home support, fall risk, and factors affecting management without asserting BPPV.

items.previous_wording: follow-up documented if arranged by clinician

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-led outcome reassessment, persistent symptom documentation, safety discussion, questions, and arranged follow-up without generating treatment or a manoeuvre.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports focal neurological and hearing red flags, acute vestibular syndrome, HINTS by trained clinicians, neuroimaging referral, and Hallpike assessment for positional vertigo.

added_items.rationale: The exact adult section supports documenting onset, episode pattern, associated hearing or neurological symptoms, relevant negatives, functional impact and clinician concern without diagnosing a vestibular or neurological cause.

added_items.final_wording: Supports documenting positional symptom context, clinician-performed diagnostic assessment, mobility, central nervous system context, home support, fall risk, and factors affecting management without asserting BPPV.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Benign Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo (Update)
source.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE vestibular and referral-pathway adaptation
source.population: Adults aged 18 years and older with suspected, diagnosed, monitored, or managed benign paroxysmal positional vertigo.
source.setting: Any clinical setting in which adult BPPV is identified, monitored, or managed.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult positional-symptom assessment, modifying factors, fall-risk context, reassessment, persistent symptoms, education, and clinician-arranged follow-up. It does not diagnose BPPV, select a manoeuvre, recommend imaging, or prescribe vestibular suppressants.

exact_location.heading: Key action statements 1a–3 — positional assessment, differential, and modifying factors

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting positional symptom context, clinician-performed diagnostic assessment, mobility, central nervous system context, home support, fall risk, and factors affecting management without asserting BPPV.

added_items.rationale: The exact BPPV section supports positional context, mobility, falls, support, and clinician-performed assessment without asserting BPPV or generating a manoeuvre.

added_items.final_wording: Supports clinician-led outcome reassessment, persistent symptom documentation, safety discussion, questions, and arranged follow-up without generating treatment or a manoeuvre.
